name: Platform Integrity Signal Decoupling
consequences: Decoupling MitID's anti-fraud model from Apple/Google platform integrity signals reduces the risk of service disruptions due to platform policy changes or geopolitical events.
options: Migrate to a decentralized, blockchain-based trust anchor that verifies identity and device integrity without relying on commercial platform providers.
Implement a hybrid approach where MitID uses both platform-controlled signals and additional, locally-hosted verification mechanisms for enhanced resilience.
Replace the current platform-integrity based model with a more traditional, centrally-managed authentication system using passwords or tokens.
review: Decoupling introduces trade-offs between security guarantees, scalability, and operational complexity – the hybrid approach may struggle to balance these competing demands.

name: Fallback Authentication Path Certification
consequences: Certifying a fallback authentication path for Danish digital identity ensures continuity of service in case of platform unavailability or policy changes.
options: Develop and certify a standards-based, open-source fallback authentication protocol that can be integrated with existing MitID systems.
Collaborate with industry partners to create a certification program for third-party fallback authentication solutions that meet specific Danish digital identity requirements.
Establish a government-led initiative to develop and certify a national fallback authentication standard, ensuring compatibility across all public sector services.
review: Certification introduces trade-offs between security standards, vendor lock-in, and procurement complexity – the industry-led approach may struggle to ensure consistency with Danish digital identity requirements.

name: Procurement Language Influence
consequences: Influencing AltID's procurement language ensures that future digital identity systems are designed with platform-neutral fallback capabilities.
options: Engage with the AltID procurement team to incorporate specific platform-neutrality and fallback access requirements into the tender documents.
Collaborate with industry partners to develop a set of best practices for designing platform-neutral digital identity systems that can be shared across multiple procurement initiatives.
Advocate for a government-led initiative to establish minimum platform-neutrality standards for all public sector digital identity procurements.
review: Influence introduces trade-offs between procurement complexity, vendor lock-in, and supplier concentration risk – the industry-led approach may struggle to ensure consistency with Danish digital identity requirements.

name: EU-Level Engagement
consequences: Engaging with EU bodies ensures that Danish advocacy aligns with broader European requirements on interoperability, portability, resilience, and reduced platform lock-in.
options: Collaborate with the European Digital Identity Wallet framework to develop a set of common standards for platform-neutral digital identity systems.
Advocate for the inclusion of platform-neutrality and fallback access language in relevant EU regulations and directives.
Establish a Danish-EU working group to coordinate advocacy efforts and ensure alignment with broader European requirements.
review: Engagement introduces trade-offs between EU bureaucratic processes, vendor lock-in, and supplier concentration risk – the collaborative approach may struggle to achieve timely consensus on platform-neutral standards.

name: Physical Location Establishment
consequences: Establishing a physical location in Copenhagen ensures access-controlled workspace for security-sensitive digital identity research, development, and stakeholder meetings.
options: Secure funding for a dedicated research facility within the University of Copenhagen or another reputable institution.
Collaborate with existing R&D facilities to establish a shared, secure workspace for project activities.
Establish a public-private partnership to create a government-sponsored innovation hub focused on digital identity and cybersecurity.
review: Establishment introduces trade-offs between physical infrastructure costs, vendor lock-in, and procurement complexity – the collaborative approach may struggle to ensure consistency with Danish digital identity requirements.

name: Regulatory Framework Update
consequences: Updating Denmark's regulatory framework ensures alignment with platform-neutral digital identity standards and requirements.
options: Advocate for updates to the eIDAS and NSIS regulations to include specific platform-neutrality and fallback access language.
Collaborate with relevant government agencies to develop a set of updated guidelines for designing platform-neutral digital identity systems.
Establish a working group with industry partners to ensure alignment between regulatory updates, procurement language, and supplier concentration risk mitigation strategies.
review: Update introduces trade-offs between regulatory complexity, vendor lock-in, and supplier concentration risk – the collaborative approach may struggle to achieve timely consensus on platform-neutral standards.

name: Stakeholder Engagement
consequences: Engaging with relevant stakeholders ensures buy-in from industry partners, government agencies, and civil society organizations.
options: Establish a stakeholder advisory board comprising representatives from key industry players, government agencies, and civil society organizations.
Collaborate with existing advocacy groups to amplify the project's message and build support among key stakeholders.
Develop a targeted outreach program to engage with specific stakeholders who can help drive change within their respective organizations or communities.
review: Engagement introduces trade-offs between stakeholder buy-in, vendor lock-in, and supplier concentration risk – the collaborative approach may struggle to ensure consistency with Danish digital identity requirements.

name: Digital Sovereignty Governance Framework
consequences: Implementing this lever will require Denmark to establish and maintain a governance framework that prioritizes digital sovereignty in its procurement processes and infrastructure decisions.
options: Embed digital sovereignty principles into national IT procurement guidelines
Establish an independent review board for evaluating the impact of foreign technology suppliers on Denmark's digital identity infrastructure
Create a digital sovereignty index to measure and compare the level of dependency on foreign technology suppliers across various government agencies
review: This lever introduces a trade-off between increased governance complexity and enhanced digital sovereignty, leaving unaddressed the challenge of balancing competing interests from different stakeholders.

name: Platform-Neutral Access Standards
consequences: Implementing this lever will require Denmark to develop and adopt platform-neutral access standards for its digital identity infrastructure, potentially leading to increased costs and complexity in implementing these standards.
options: Develop open standards for authentication and authorization that are not tied to specific platforms
Create a certification program for vendors that meet the platform-neutral access standards
Establish a technical working group to oversee the development and implementation of platform-neutral access standards
review: This lever introduces a trade-off between increased standardization complexity and enhanced digital sovereignty, leaving unaddressed the challenge of ensuring compatibility with existing systems.

name: Suppliers' Risk Assessment and Mitigation
consequences: Implementing this lever will require Denmark to conduct thorough risk assessments and develop mitigation strategies for its reliance on foreign technology suppliers, potentially leading to increased costs and complexity in implementing these measures.
options: Conduct regular risk assessments of the impact of foreign technology suppliers on Denmark's digital identity infrastructure
Develop a contingency plan for critical services in case of supplier disruption or withdrawal
Establish a dedicated team to monitor and address potential risks associated with foreign technology suppliers
review: This lever introduces a trade-off between increased risk management complexity and enhanced digital sovereignty, leaving unaddressed the challenge of balancing competing interests from different stakeholders.

name: Stakeholder Engagement Strategy
consequences: Implementing this lever will require Denmark to develop and implement an effective stakeholder engagement strategy to ensure broad support for its digital identity infrastructure improvements.
options: Establish a dedicated team to engage with key stakeholders, including civil society organizations and industry experts
Develop a comprehensive communication plan to raise awareness about the importance of digital sovereignty
Create a stakeholder advisory board to provide input on the development of digital sovereignty policies
review: This lever introduces a trade-off between increased stakeholder engagement complexity and enhanced digital sovereignty, leaving unaddressed the challenge of ensuring that all stakeholders are aligned with Denmark's goals.

name: Technical Demonstrator Development
consequences: Implementing this lever will require Denmark to develop technical demonstrators for its platform-neutral access standards, potentially leading to increased costs and complexity in implementing these demonstrations.
options: Develop a mobile-oriented demonstrator for authenticating against a simulated Danish public-sector identity-provider environment
Create a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path demonstrator that reduces dependence on the mobile OS duopoly
Establish a technical working group to oversee the development and implementation of technical demonstrators
review: This lever introduces a trade-off between increased standardization complexity and enhanced digital sovereignty, leaving unaddressed the challenge of ensuring compatibility with existing systems.
